purpose: Section-specific rubric for scoring a draft principle's gates field. Six dimensions, 0-3 scale, threshold = all ≥ 2. Composed at runtime with system_prompts/rubric.json.
history: Authored 2026-05-27 alongside GO1B1-05's gates section. Calibration corpus: GO1B1-01 / 02 / 03 / 04 gates sections in principles.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE SCORING: gates

Score the candidate gates array against the six dimensions below.

DIMENSIONS

1. lifecycle_point_valid — point is from the enums (pre_commit, pre_merge, pre_deploy, release, quarterly_review, annual_review, incident_response) AND is appropriate for the principle's ownership tier. Project-tier → pre_merge/pre_deploy. Enterprise-tier → release/quarterly_review/annual_review.
   - 3: All gates use valid enums; tier-appropriate.
   - 2: All valid but one gate's point is slightly off-tier (e.g. project gate at release).
   - 1: One gate uses an invalid enum or a gate is clearly wrong-tier.
   - 0: Multiple gates use invalid enums or wrong-tier placement.

2. check_is_binary_and_verifiable — Each check describes something mechanically verifiable. Not recommendation language.
   - 3: Every check is a verifiable condition (file exists, field present, count ≥ N, PR diff matches pattern).
   - 2: Most checks verifiable; one slightly ambiguous.
   - 1: One check reads as a recommendation ('should', 'ensure', 'verify quality').
   - 0: Multiple checks are recommendations rather than verifiable conditions.

3. required_status_check_constraint_present — For project-tier CI-enforced gates, the check text explicitly states it is configured as a required status check on the integration branch, that advisory CI does not satisfy the gate, and what failure blocks. The catalogue convention uses this verbatim: 'Configured as a required status check on the integration branch (typically `develop` in git-flow setups, `main` in trunk-based setups) via branch protection — advisory CI runs do not satisfy this gate.'
   - 3: Every project-tier CI gate carries the full branch-protection constraint with the standard phrasing.
   - 2: Constraint present but slightly compressed or only on some of the project-tier gates.
   - 1: Constraint present but watered down ('runs on CI' without naming required status check).
   - 0: No branch-protection constraint on project-tier CI gates — the most common failure mode.

4. blocking_correctness — The blocking boolean is correct. CI-enforced project-tier gates are almost always blocking: true.
   - 3: All blocking values correct.
   - 2: All correct but a borderline-advisory gate flagged blocking (or vice versa).
   - 1: One gate has incorrect blocking value.
   - 0: Multiple incorrect blocking values.

5. coverage_completeness — Gates collectively enforce every artefact named in solution.approach.
   - 3: Every solution.approach artefact is enforced by at least one gate.
   - 2: Most artefacts covered; one minor artefact unenforced (e.g. a log file with no gate).
   - 1: Multiple solution.approach artefacts have no gate.
   - 0: Gates enforce something solution doesn't name, or major solution artefacts are uncovered.

6. parallel_form_with_siblings — Gate count (1-3), structure { point, check, blocking }, branch-protection boilerplate phrasing, vocabulary match siblings.
   - 3: Form indistinguishable from sibling gates sections.
   - 2: Minor variance (slightly longer check text; one extra gate) but recognisably the same family.
   - 1: Off-form on one axis (4+ gates without strong reason, missing branch-protection boilerplate).
   - 0: Reads as authored against a different template.

THRESHOLD

Draft passes if all six dimensions score ≥ 2. A score of 0 or 1 on any dimension fails the rubric and routes the draft back to revise.
user_template:
Score this candidate gates array against the six dimensions.

<ownership_tier>{{ ownership_tier }}</ownership_tier>

<aws_anchor>
  <best_practice>{{ aws_bp }}</best_practice>
  <implementation_step>
    <number>{{ aws_step_number }}</number>
    <title>{{ aws_step_title }}</title>
    <verbatim_text>{{ aws_step_verbatim }}</verbatim_text>
  </implementation_step>
</aws_anchor>

<statement>
  <title>{{ statement_title }}</title>
  <description>{{ statement_description }}</description>
</statement>

<solution>
  <approach>{{ solution_approach }}</approach>
</solution>

<siblings_under_same_bp>
{{ sibling_gates }}
</siblings_under_same_bp>

<candidate>
  <gates>{{ candidate_gates }}</gates>
</candidate>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.

calibration_examples.note: Two pre_merge gates: existence of eval/scenarios/ + eval/data/, and harness-pass on PRs touching src/agent/. Both carry full branch-protection language.
Existence + per-stratum threshold gate, and COVERAGE gate coupling agent-code changes to strata-manifest changes.
Existence of eval/metrics/ with named metric, and lint forbidding metric-shaped definitions outside eval/metrics/.
Existence + completeness of eval/drift/config.yaml, and baseline-update coupling on model-changing PRs.
